@hastinapura.Name: Hastinapura
@hastinapura.Notes: Primary capital of the Kuru kingdom. Archaeological site at Hastinapur village, Meerut.

@indraprastha.Name: Indraprastha
@indraprastha.Notes: Built by Maya Danava for Pandavas on Khandava land. Identified with Purana Qila, Delhi.

@panchala.Name: Panchala
@panchala.Modern_Equivalent: Ahichchhatra (North Panchala), Kampilya (South Panchala), Uttar Pradesh
@panchala.Notes: Divided into North Panchala (capital Ahichchhatra) and South Panchala (capital Kampilya). Archaeological ruins at both sites.

@dvaraka.Name: Dvaraka
@dvaraka.Notes: Built by Vishwakarma; sank into sea after Mausala. Underwater ruins found by ASI off Dwarka coast.

@magadha.Name: Magadha
@magadha.Notes: Powerful eastern kingdom opposing Yadavas. Capital at Rajgir (Girivraja).

@kashi.Name: Kashi
@kashi.Notes: Princesses Amba, Ambika, Ambalika abducted from here by Bhishma

@chedi.Name: Chedi
Famous_For: Shishupala killed by Krishna at Rajasuya
@chedi.Notes: Capital identified with Suktimati or Sothivatinagara.

@anga.Name: Anga
@anga.Notes: Duryodhana crowned Karna king of Anga. Capital at Champa (modern Bhagalpur).

@gandhara.Name: Gandhara
@gandhara.Notes: Capital at Takshashila (Taxila). One of the sixteen Mahajanapadas.

@viratanagara.Name: Viratanagara
Famous_For: Pandavas served in disguise for one year
@viratanagara.Notes: Archaeological remains found at Bairat. Circular temple and Ashokan edict.

@trigarta.Name: Trigarta
@trigarta.Notes: Name means 'land between three rivers' (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej).

@vidarbha.Name: Vidarbha
@vidarbha.Notes: Capital identified with Kundina (modern Kaundinyapur near Amravati).

@kunti_kingdom.Name: Kunti
@kunti_kingdom.Notes: Kingdom of Kuntibhoja where Pritha (Kunti) grew up. Identified with area around Bundi.

@kurukshetra.Name: Kurukshetra
@kurukshetra.Notes: Dharmakshetra - field of righteousness. Jyotisar - exact spot of Gita Upadesh.

@khandava_forest.Name: Khandava Forest
@khandava_forest.Notes: Agni consumed this forest with help of Arjuna and Krishna. Region south of Indraprastha.

@kamyaka_forest.Name: Kamyaka Forest
@kamyaka_forest.Notes: Main forest during Pandavas' 12-year exile. Identified near banks of old Sarasvati.

@dvaitavana.Name: Dvaitavana
@dvaitavana.Notes: Forest of exile near a sacred lake. Located close to Kurukshetra.

@naimisha_forest.Name: Naimisha Forest
@naimisha_forest.Notes: Where the outer frame story of Mahabharata is set. Active pilgrimage site today.

@ganga_river.Name: Ganga

Geography.Terrain: Major river flowing east to Bay of Bengal

@ganga_river.Notes: Personified as goddess Ganga, mother of Bhishma. Shantanu met her near Hastinapura.

@yamuna_river.Name: Yamuna
@yamuna_river.Modern_Equivalent: Yamuna at Delhi (flows past Purana Qila/Indraprastha)
@yamuna_river.Notes: Also known as Kalindi. Flows past Indraprastha (Delhi), Mathura, and Vrindavana.

@sarasvati_river.Name: Sarasvati
@sarasvati_river.Modern_Equivalent: Dried Sarasvati channel near Kurukshetra/Pehowa, Haryana
@sarasvati_river.Notes: Mentioned as flowing near Kurukshetra. Dried channel identified via satellite imagery.

@himalayas.Name: Himalayas
@himalayas.Notes: Site of Arjuna's tapas, Pandavas' ascent in Mahaprasthanika Parva

@meru.Name: Mount Meru
@meru.Notes: Cosmic mountain at center of universe in Hindu cosmology. No physical location.

@gandhamadana.Name: Gandhamadana
@gandhamadana.Notes: Bhima encountered Hanuman while searching for Saugandhika lotus

@kailasa.Name: Kailasa
@kailasa.Notes: Mount Kailash - sacred abode of Lord Shiva. Active pilgrimage destination.

@svarga.Name: Svarga
@svarga.Notes: Indra's heaven; Arjuna spent time here learning weapons and dance

@amaravati.Name: Amaravati
@amaravati.Notes: City of the gods in Indra's heaven

@ekachakra.Name: Ekachakra
@ekachakra.Notes: Pandavas stayed here as Brahmins after Lakshagriha escape

@upaplavya.Name: Upaplavya
@upaplavya.Notes: City in Matsya where Pandavas gathered allies before war

@mathura.Name: Mathura
@mathura.Notes: Krishna was born here in Kamsa's prison. Keshava Deo temple at birthplace.

@shatashriga.Name: Shatashriga
@shatashriga.Notes: Mountain where Pandu lived in exile and died. Identified with Shivalik foothills.

@lakshagriha.Name: Lakshagriha
@lakshagriha.Notes: Also called Varanavata; trap set for Pandavas. Lacquer mound (Laksha ka Tila) survives at Barnawa.
